_comment: Copy this to config.json and paste your two free keys in. Both take about two minutes to get and neither ever expires or charges.
_pexels: Get a key at https://www.pexels.com/api/ (200 requests per hour, free forever)
_pixabay: Get a key at https://pixabay.com/api/docs/ (100 requests per minute, free forever)
_contact: Optional. A URL or email that identifies you to the public archives. Wikimedia requires contact details in the User-Agent and blocks requests without them. The default points at the project repo, which is fine for personal use.
_smithsonian: Optional. Free key at https://api.data.gov/signup/ — Smithsonian already works without one on a shared DEMO_KEY rate limit; a key of your own just raises the ceiling.
_europeana: Optional. Free key at https://pro.europeana.eu/pages/get-api — adds 4,000 European museums and libraries. Only their CC0 and Public Domain Mark material is used.
_gemini: Optional. Free key at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey — only needed if you want to generate sheets from a script (and you're using llm=gemini).
_gemini_model: "auto" asks your key which models it can call and picks the best Flash one. Only pin a name here if you want a specific model.
_llm: Which language model writes the sheets, image queries and descriptions. One of: "gemini" (cloud, needs gemini_key), "ollama" (a model on your OWN machine — free, private), "grok" (xAI, needs grok_key), or "openrouter" (many models behind one key, incl. free ones). Switch per machine — nothing else in the pipeline changes. Voice, visuals, captions and audio are unaffected.
_ollama_host: Where Ollama listens. The default is right unless you changed it.
_ollama_model: The local model to use when llm=ollama, e.g. qwen3:14b or glm-4.7-flash. Pull it first: `ollama pull <name>`. A strong instruction-follower (14B+) is recommended — sheet generation checks the script is reproduced word-for-word and will retry a weaker model.
_grok: xAI Grok, used only when llm=grok. Get a key at https://console.x.ai. Grok speaks the OpenAI-compatible API, so any current model name works.
_grok_model: The Grok model to use, e.g. grok-4 or grok-4-fast. Set the name your account can call.
_openrouter: Many models behind one key, used only when llm=openrouter. Free key at https://openrouter.ai/keys. Models whose id ends in :free cost nothing (rate-limited: ~50 requests/day on a $0 balance, 1000/day once you've added $10+ of credit).
_openrouter_model: Which model to route to, e.g. deepseek/deepseek-r1:free or qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct:free for no cost, or any paid id. Pick a strong instruction-follower for sheet generation.
_clip: Visual matching: scores each candidate picture for how well it actually matches the scene, not just its size. Free and local (open weights on your own GPU/CPU, one-time download). "auto" turns it on wherever it can run; set "off" to rank by size and aspect only.
_clip_model: Optional override of the matcher, e.g. openai/clip-vit-base-patch32 or google/siglip2-so400m-patch14-384. Empty lets it auto-pick for the machine: SigLIP 2 on a real GPU (needs transformers >= 4.49), dependable CLIP on Apple/CPU. Anything that won't load falls back to CLIP automatically.
_clip_min: How good a match must be before sourcing stops searching (0-1). Lower accepts looser matches; higher searches harder and flags more scenes. 0.45 is a sensible start.
_source_log: How much per-scene detail the live Output shows while sourcing. Default (any value but "full") gives a clean result line plus the top few candidates. Set "full" to list EVERY scored candidate for every scene — the complete firehose.
_expand_queries: Uses your Gemini key to generate a few concrete alternative image searches per scene, so a weak or abstract line (e.g. a 'hypnogram') still finds a real photo. "auto" is on when a Gemini key is set; "off" uses only the queries in the sheet. Cached per project, so a re-source costs nothing.
_align: Word-by-word caption timing. "auto" uses torchaudio's built-in forced alignment (free, local, on your GPU, NO extra install — it rides the same torchaudio the voice engine uses) so each spoken word lights up in time; "off" falls back to estimated timing from word length. Captions render either way.
_audio_master: Loudness mastering. "auto" brings every finished video to YouTube's -14 LUFS standard so it plays back at the same volume as everything else; "off" leaves the raw mix.
_lufs_target: Target integrated loudness in LUFS. -14 is YouTube standard; -16 is a touch quieter (closer to podcast/TV).
_music_duck: When true, background music automatically ducks (drops back) under the narration and swells in the gaps, so speech is always clear. false keeps a flat music level.
